rulings.Case Title: CARROLL CLINE et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Albay Construction Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgement, Or In The Alternatice Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs¿ strict liability and false representation causes of action is off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cline was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Cline¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Cline. By failing to demonstrate that plaintiffs submitted factually devoid responses to comprehensive contention discovery designed to elicit all evidence and information plaintiffs have to support their contention that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Cline, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their strict liability and false representation causes of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CARROLL CLINE et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Dillingham Construction, N.A., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied. On the causation issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Cline¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Cline was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. On the duty issue, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it owed no duty to Mr. Cline. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CARROLL CLINE et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Boss Manufacturing Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Boss Manufacturing Company¿s motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication is continued on the court¿s own motion to January 21, 2014 due to defendant¿s failure to provide the Court with a complete set of its moving papers. No later than 4:00p.m. on January 15, 2014, defendant must deliver to Department 503 a complete copy of the declaration of Lisa Corman with all exhibits attached. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CARROLL CLINE et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Goulds Pumps, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cline was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Cline¿s deposition testimony and plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES DEL PINO VS. 3M COMPANY INDIVIDUALLY AND FKA MINNESOTA,
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 15, 2014 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Assuming that Mr. Del Pino was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant, plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Del Pino¿s mesothelioma. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
